own_review.issue: If two NAR files declare the same function name, the second next.put(name, ...) silently overwrites the first archive, which is then neither in the active map nor in functionsToClose -> classloader/file-handle leak. The old full-reload code closed every prior archive. Same latent flaw exists in ConnectorUtils.reloadConnectors, but for functions this leak is newly introduced by this PR.

reloadFunctions is an unsynchronized read-modify-write on this.functions; two concurrent reload calls (admin REST endpoint) can double-close the same evicted archives and leak the losing thread's newly created archives. volatile only fixes visibility, not atomicity. Pre-existing race in the old code and mirrored from ConnectorsManager, so not a blocker for this PR.

Constructor now performs full-file I/O (MD5 of the NAR) and can throw UncheckedIOException — a contract change for existing callers. In-repo callers are safe (searchForFunctions catches Throwable, which silently skips the function on hash failure; the test helper uses a real file), but every scan now reads each NAR fully for hashing — extra startup/reload cost for large built-in NARs.

computeArchiveMd5Hex/calculateMd5Hex are verbatim duplicates of the public ConnectorUtils.computeArchiveMd5Hex (ConnectorUtils.java:61-66); should be extracted to a shared utility instead of duplicated.
Unlike searchForFunctions, a missing functions directory no longer logs a warning; all archives are silently closed.
Temp directories/NAR extraction dirs are never cleaned up (no @AfterMethod); no test covers a brand-new NAR appearing between scans.

points.point: volatile on functions field correct, consistent with ConnectorsManager; 'no additional synchronization needed'
points.note: Accurate praise on visibility, but glosses over the (pre-existing) reload read-modify-write race it claims away.

points.point: reloadFunctions delegation matches ConnectorsManager pattern; old code closed all archives
closeFunctions overload refactor is clean (Collection vs Map+clear)
@UtilityClass: verify methods are static or 'compilation will fail'
points.note: False: Lombok @UtilityClass automatically marks members static; non-static members do not fail compilation. Also moot — the diff's methods are explicitly static.

points.point: Reuse ConnectorUtils.computeArchiveMd5Hex / extract shared MD5 logic instead of duplicating
points.note: Verified: ConnectorUtils.java:61-66 has identical helpers; matches the human reviewer's sole point.

points.point: archiveMd5Hex should be computed at construction, not lazily
points.note: The diff already computes it in the constructor; vacuous advice from the truncated view, not a false assertion.

points.point: Test suite covers four scenarios; consider concurrent-reload test
points.note: Scenarios exist but in FunctionUtilsReloadTest, not the manager test it cites; suggestion is mild.

missed: Duplicate function names across NARs leak the overwritten archive (never closed) — new regression vs old close-everything reload
FunctionArchive constructor now does file I/O and throws UncheckedIOException — contract change / silent fail-open skip in searchForFunctions

points.point: Missing test for a new NAR added between scans
points.note: Verified: the four tests cover unchanged/changed/removed/missing-dir only; the new-function branch is untested.

points.point: computeArchiveMd5Hex/calculateMd5Hex indirection duplicated from ConnectorUtils; cross-cutting cleanup
points.note: Verified duplication of ConnectorUtils.java:61-66; covers the human reviewer's shared-utils point.

points.point: Constructor wraps IOException in UncheckedIOException; searchForFunctions catches Throwable and silently skips — fail-open, callers never see the IOException
points.note: Verified against searchForFunctions catch(Throwable); matches my own finding.

points.point: Swap-then-close ordering means brief coexistence of old+new archives; consistent with ConnectorsManager
points.note: Accurate, self-labelled as observation not concern.

missed: Duplicate function names across NARs leak the overwritten archive (never closed) — new regression vs old close-everything reload
exceeds: Missing new-NAR test case (beyond human reference)
UncheckedIOException fail-open behavior in searchForFunctions (beyond human reference)

qualitative: v5 is clearly stronger: every one of its four points is verified-true against the repo, three are substantive (untested new-NAR path, MD5-helper duplication mirroring the human's only comment, and the UncheckedIOException fail-open contract change my own review also flagged), and it correctly self-calibrates the fourth as a non-concern. v4 is dominated by accurate but non-actionable praise of the diff, hedged speculation driven by its truncated view (advice to do things the code already does), and one factually wrong claim about Lombok @UtilityClass requiring explicit static members; its single solid point is the MD5 duplication, which overlaps the human reference. Both candidates missed the one genuine regression I found: when two NARs declare the same function name, the overwritten FunctionArchive is neither kept nor closed, leaking a classloader — behavior the old close-everything reload did not have. Neither candidate fabricated absent code, though v4's Lombok claim is a false technical assertion.
